In the 1950s, Britain had a massive railway network. A decade later it was barely recognisable. For better and for worse, this is the story of how and why our railways were changed forever.

programmes.show_description: A look at the extraordinary popularity of both Hitler and the Nazis and the astonishing degree of voluntary support that ordinary Germans gave to the infamous Gestapo. Ep2: Chaos and Consent

The 1917 revolution changed Russia nearly overnight, but Communism did not immediately follow the overthrow of the Tsar. Instead, chaos ensued, as Lenin maintained control at all costs. E2

The incredible story of the Avro Lancaster, one of the finest bombers of the Second World War which played a crucial role in the long and savage campaign to defeat Hitler's Third Reich.

programmes.show_name: Krays: The Men Behind the Myth
programmes.show_description: Ronnie and Reggie Kray came from humble beginnings to become the UK's most notorious and feared criminals - we look at how they built and ran their criminal empire before being brought to justice.
...Attempted Murder of Beatle George Harrison, George Best's Alcoholism, Heaven's Gate Mass Suicide and Fatty Arbuckle, the Star in a Murder Trial. The crimes examined in this episode of Crime Inc.

...You? There are some news events that change the world - in this episode, we take you back to April 1986 and the Chernobyl disaster and its wider implications.
Survivors recount the horrors behind surviving a merciless place and hear the shock of Allied soldiers as they discover the evil secret of Germany's concentration camps. Ep11: Fall of Berlin
Veterans reveal the real reasons they joined the fighting forces. They tell of inadequate training, of meeting the enemy, and the shocking realities of combat and capture. Ep1: Capture
